topic: Braintrust Review 2026: AI Observability, Evals & Production Monitoring

key_points: LangChain's commercial observability platform with zero-config tracing for LangChain apps
Deep LangChain/LangGraph integration but creates vendor lock-in
Per-trace pricing scales exponentially with traffic volume
Structured evaluation and visibility but acting on results requires manual review
Best for teams already heavily invested in the LangChain ecosystem
Open-source (MIT) LLM observability platform — no vendor lock-in
Built around ClickHouse for analytical query engine
Self-hosting requires running 5+ services (ClickHouse, PostgreSQL, etc.)
Widest framework integration coverage among open-source tools
Best for teams prioritizing open-source infrastructure control over evaluation depth
Requires manual authoring for eval generation from production data
Enterprise-grade AI observability and monitoring platform
Supports traditional ML and LLM monitoring in one platform
Strong on drift detection and model performance monitoring
Steeper learning curve than Braintrust
Better suited for ML Ops teams vs pure LLM teams
Focused specifically on LLM evaluation (DeepEval framework)
Strong open-source community around DeepEval
Less full-featured on the observability/tracing side vs Braintrust
Good for teams wanting evaluation-first approach with open-source tooling
Agent-first observability platform targeting production multi-turn agents
Emerging competitor with focus on agent workflows
Smaller ecosystem and customer base compared to Braintrust
Positioned for teams running complex agentic systems

stats.fact: Braintrust raised $80M Series B in February 2026 at an $800M valuation
Total Braintrust funding: $242.5M across 7 funding rounds (as of early 2026)
Series B led by ICONIQ with participation from Andreessen Horowitz, Greylock, and Elad Gil
Brainstore (Braintrust's purpose-built database) is 80x faster than traditional data warehouses on real-world benchmarks with median query times under one second
Notion went from catching 3 issues per day to 30 after adopting Braintrust — a 10x improvement
Enterprise customers include Notion, Stripe, Vercel, Airtable, Instacart, Zapier, Ramp, Dropbox, Cloudflare, and BILL
Average AI trace span is ~50KB vs ~900 bytes in traditional observability — Brainstore handles this scale with queries in seconds
Free tier: 1M trace spans + 10k evaluation scores per month with unlimited users
Pro plan: $249/month with unlimited spans and scores
Braintrust SDKs support 13+ frameworks including OpenAI Agents SDK, LangGraph, Mastra, Pydantic AI, LangChain, CrewAI, and Vercel AI SDK

angles: The unified platform advantage: why combining evals + tracing + dataset curation in one tool beats stitching together separate solutions
Braintrust's $800M bet on being the 'observability layer for AI' — is the moat real or marketing?
Free tier vs $249/month Pro: who actually needs to upgrade and when?
Brainstore's 80x speed advantage: does it matter in practice for teams not at Notion/Stripe scale?
Loop (AI assistant for evals): the most underrated feature in the Braintrust ecosystem
Braintrust vs open-source (Langfuse): the build-vs-buy decision in 2026
The proprietary SaaS concern: no self-hosting and no real exit path
CI/CD integration for AI quality: how Braintrust blocks deploys when quality degrades
outline: H2: What Is Braintrust? The AI Observability Platform Explained
H2: Core Features: Tracing, Evals, Datasets, and Loop
H2: Braintrust Pricing in 2026: Free, Pro, and Enterprise Tiers
H2: Braintrust vs LangSmith vs Langfuse: Which Should You Choose?
H2: Real-World Results: How Notion, Stripe, and Vercel Use Braintrust
H2: Limitations and Honest Criticisms of Braintrust
H2: Who Should Use Braintrust (and Who Should Not)
H2: Final Verdict: Is Braintrust Worth It in 2026?
